note: Submodule of acquia_search. Ships only optional config (a default Search API Solr index + a search View); no code, settings, permissions or Drush. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded), probing documented facts; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: What does the Acquia Search Defaults submodule give you, and what does enabling it require?
evals.expected_output: It installs a ready-made Search API Solr index (acquia_search_index) on the acquia_search_server plus a search View (views.view.acquia_search), so a connected site has working node search out of the box. It ships only optional config — no code, settings, permissions or Drush.

evals.prompt: Which config entities does Acquia Search Defaults create, and what server does the index attach to?
evals.expected_output: A Search API index 'acquia_search_index' attached to the 'acquia_search_server', and a View 'views.view.acquia_search'.

evals.prompt: Why might enabling Acquia Search Defaults not actually create its index? What does it depend on?
evals.expected_output: Its config lives in config/optional, so the index is imported only if its dependencies already exist — the acquia_search_server and the node Body field (field.storage.node.body). If those are missing at enable time, Drupal silently skips creating the index.
